verdict: EMPTY (SEARCH-METHOD LIMIT) — the (circular-body Tisserand seed + local TOF optimiser + multi-rev Lambert) method finds no ballistic-gate-passing precursor-MGA into either classic E-M cycler under real DE440 ephemeris; NOT proof of non-existence
interpretation: max_revs=2 IS active (validated median flyby-continuity drops ~2 km/s vs single-rev #302) but the two binding residuals never co-minimise: the best joint candidate (E-V-E) carries closure~3.05 + continuity~4.0 km/s on both targets, both an order of magnitude above the 0.10 km/s ballistic gate. A leg can reach near-ballistic continuity only at the cost of a ~4 km/s terminal V_inf-mismatch. IMPORTANT precision: the closure (close_epoch_locked) already uses real DE440 ephemeris (eccentric bodies); the circular-body assumption is only in the Tisserand SEED enumerator, and the TOF refinement is LOCAL Nelder-Mead. So this is a search-method limit (circular seeds + local optimiser cannot reach a real-eph ballistic basin), NOT proof the cyclers lack a ballistic precursor. reverification.reason: the coplanar-prograde model is GENEROUS to Triton, not conservative: at the model's generous v_inf range (1.0-2.0 km/s) bend is 23-59 deg, but correcting Triton's true retrograde rotation (v_moon reverses sign) forces v_inf up to ~8.5-8.7 km/s via the equal-magnitude chord identity |v_sc-v_moon| vs |v_sc+v_moon|, collapsing bend to ~1.46-1.52 deg. interpretation: BOTH cross-system connection legs exist at low dV: forward EM-L2->SE-L2 closes at pos gap ~0.4 km / dV ~0.36 km/s, and the SE->EM RETURN leg closes at pos gap ~30 km / dV ~0.15 km/s (validated by test_se_to_em_return_leg_converges). With each manifold propagated in its own system the return converges. What remains OPEN is the closed CYCLE: a theta-time-consistency analysis quantifies the gate. The forward EM-L2->SE-L2 transit takes ~52 days, over which the SE-EM relative phase advances ~4.88 rad; the time-consistent prediction does NOT match the independently-converged return-leg theta -- a ~2.2 rad gap at single revolution. Both legs stay ballistic-cheap (~0.8 km/s total) throughout -- the obstruction is phase-closure DIMENSIONALITY, not energy. RESWEEP CONDITION (verbatim, NOT more revolutions / finer grids): (1) other libration pairs (L1/L1, L1/L2) -- may give a 2-D both-converged region = two real amplitude knobs (UNTESTED); (2) BCR4BP with an SE-scale seed (#412 re-scope), where phase closure is well-posed by construction. For EM-L1 Lyapunov seeds (C=3.1294/3.05/2.95) cast into the BCR4BP and mu_sun-continued, the max Earth-relative reach stays ~0.9-1.16 lunar distances (LD) -- flat under the Sun term, nowhere near the 3.90 LD needed to span to the Sun-Earth-L region (1.5e6 km patch plane). verdict: EMPTY for close-Jupiter-encounter cycler structure -- stable periodic-orbit backbone recovered, none enters the Hill sphere
interpretation: Positive control PASSED (recovered a low-residual candidate at the Kepler-derived Hilda seed, C=3.0613, before the full scan ran) so this negative is trustworthy, not a search-configuration artefact. The scan certified 33 real periodic-orbit family members (residuals ~1e-14, well-converged) across a Jacobi band bracketing the Hilda 3:2 interior MMR, all independently grounded: the Hilda semi-major axis (3.97 AU) was derived from Kepler III on Jupiter's sourced SMA and matches the real, independently-documented Hilda asteroid group; the back-derived GM_sun matched the IAU-standard value exactly; the computed Jupiter Hill radius (0.355 AU) matches the well-known literature value. BUT: the closest approach to Jupiter found across all 33 certified orbits (0.1265 normalized, 98.5 million km) sits at 1.85 Hill radii -- safely OUTSIDE Jupiter's sphere of gravitational dominance. This is physically expected, not a search failure: STABLE 3:2-resonance librators (the Hilda group's defining trait) are protected from close Jupiter encounters BY the resonance -- that is precisely why the group is dynamically stable and long-lived. The #527 proposal's premise ("Jupiter itself does the gravitational structuring at each pass") does not hold for this STABLE periodic-orbit backbone. The Guido & Efthymiopoulos (arXiv:2604.00679) heteroclinic-transport structures this proposal was grounded in describe a DIFFERENT dynamical object: chaotic resonance-hopping trajectories shadowing the UNSTABLE manifolds of separatrix-adjacent periodic orbits, not the stable resonant orbits themselves. A DA/HOTM periodic-orbit enumeration (this method) structurally cannot surface that -- it needs invariant-manifold computation near the unstable/separatrix orbits instead. Resweep condition: NOT more Jacobi/grid resolution on this stable-orbit search; a genuinely different method (unstable manifold propagation from the resonance separatrix) would be required to test the actual chaotic-transport cycler hypothesis.

verdict: EMPTY for linear-regime unstable-manifold-mediated Hill-sphere encounters, within the tested horizon
interpretation: POSITIVE CONTROL for the machinery PASSED universally: every one of the 9 re-certified orbits has det(monodromy)=1.000000 to displayed precision (symplectic, Liouville-consistent) and carries the expected trivial +1 eigenvalue pair -- the monodromy/eigen-decomposition machinery is verified correct independent of any physical claim. STABILITY RESULT: 8 of 9 orbits (all 3 Sun-Earth co-orbital, 5 of 6 Sun-Jupiter Hilda) are linearly stable/marginally-stable (leading non-trivial multiplier on the unit circle) -- NO unstable manifold exists for these at all, consistent with #523/#527s finding that the coarse enumerator preferentially surfaces well-behaved (and thus typically stable) family members. Exactly ONE genuinely unstable orbit was found (Sun-Jupiter Hilda, C=3.14, x0~=0.7615, lambda~=1.718, period~=6.39 nondim ~=1.0 yr). Its manifold was propagated for a Lyapunov-SCALED horizon (20 periods, computed from ln(target_growth/eps)/ln(|lambda|) so the eps=1e-6 perturbation genuinely grows to O(0.05) -- NOT a fixed guessed duration; an earlier version of this analysis used a fixed 3 periods and found IDENTICAL min_dist to baseline, which was a design bug (insufficient growth time), not a real result, caught and fixed before this entry was registered. With the corrected horizon, BOTH manifold branches (+/- eigenvector sign) still land at essentially the same closest approach as the periodic orbit itself (3.47-3.48x the Hill radius vs baseline 3.48x -- a <0.4% shift), nowhere near the Hill sphere. CAVEAT (the honest scope limit): this tests the LOCAL/LINEAR-REGIME manifold only -- a fixed eigenvector direction scaled by the growing Floquet factor, valid while the true nonlinear trajectory stays close to the linear approximation (roughly the first ~20-25 e-foldings here). It does NOT test full nonlinear manifold globalization (re-correcting the manifold onto the true invariant surface at each step, e.g. Koon-Lo-Marsden-Ross-style), which is the technique that would actually test the Guido-Efthymiopoulos heteroclinic-transport hypothesis at large distances from the parent orbit -- a materially different and more expensive method, not attempted here.

moon's own min-achievable V_inf (Hohmann-transfer tangential minimum-energy bound, the single MOST FAVORABLE-to-feasibility conic connecting the moon's orbit radius to Titan's) vs the moon's own 5 deg bend ceiling
verdict: ANALYTICALLY EMPTY -- Titan-Mimas fails the two-sided #324 physical max-bend gate at EVERY possible grid point (the moon's own encounter can never clear 5 deg bend at any V_inf reachable by a Lambert arc to Titan's radius). No compute spent sweeping.
interpretation:
Mimas's own 5-deg-bend V_inf ceiling is 0.4290 km/s (GM=2.503 km^3/s^2, safe periapsis radius = radius_eq + 100.0 km safe_alt, both from core/satellites.py, JPL SSD-sourced). The MINIMUM achievable V_inf at Mimas, over ANY conic connecting Mimas's orbit (a=185540.0 km) to Titan's orbit (a=1221870.0 km) about Saturn (GM=37931207.0 km^3/s^2), is 4.5425 km/s -- realized by the Hohmann-transfer tangential minimum-energy ellipse, the single most favorable-to-feasibility geometry (any non-tangential Lambert arc the actual sweep would evaluate has a LARGER velocity mismatch with the moon's local circular velocity, so this is a strict floor, not an approximation). At this best-case V_inf, the achievable bend is only 0.0466 deg -- 10.6x above the ceiling V_inf, so nowhere near the 5 deg floor. Because the #558-corrected genome's residual/gate structure evaluates the SAME two body-radius pair (Titan's orbit, this moon's orbit) at every (rel_offset, phase0, tof_scale, n_rev) grid point -- only the RELATIVE GEOMETRY (which V_inf, at which angle, the sweep's dimensions vary) changes, never the pair of orbit radii the Lambert arc must connect -- this Hohmann floor bounds every grid point uniformly: no point in the full (rel_offset x phase0 x tof_scale x n_rev) grid can achieve a lower V_inf at Mimas than this analytic floor, so none can clear the gate either. This is a genuine physical limit (mass-deficiency), consistent with #489's prior finding that these same four moons already fail an equivalent gate for OTHER pairings (Tethys 0.44 deg, Dione ~3.1-3.2 deg max-bend at their #489-era V_inf regimes) -- this #571 stamp extends that same physical conclusion to the specific Titan-paired case, not a re-litigation of it.

CAVEAT (conditional on this project's own gate POLICY, not a universal physical claim): this is conditional on the TWO-SIDED gate this project's genome enforces (every encounter, including the small moon's own, must bend usefully). Russell & Strange (2009)'s own published Titan-Enceladus census (19 members) uses Enceladus as a PASSIVE SCIENCE TARGET, not as a trajectory-bending flyby node -- an architecture where the small moon's encounter does NOT need to clear a bend-usefulness floor at all, because Titan alone does the trajectory work and the small-moon pass is opportunistic science, not propulsion. That is a STRUCTURALLY DIFFERENT, uncovered case for this genome (which by construction requires every node in the cycle to contribute useful bending), not a contradiction of this empty-region finding.

verdict: EMPTY for inclined, repeating Titan-Iapetus symmetric quasi_cyclers REACHABLE BY CONTINUATION from the coplanar symmetric family. Nine genuine coplanar symmetric closures exist in the idealized circular-coplanar model (verified via the C1 bit-for-bit Uranian golden and the C2 two-sided repeat control), but 0/6 (Omega+tof-free continuation) and 0/5 (zero-tof-drift Omega-only) survive extension to Iapetus's real ~15.5deg mutual inclination as a repeating cycle. The coplanar model was never a good starting point for this pair (see interpretation). This is a materially earlier and more fundamental breakdown than #574's eccentricity result: the family dies under inclination ALONE, before real eccentricity is even introduced.
interpretation: The whole #571->#575 chain was gated on whether a #552-class 3D/inclined moontour build is worth scoping for Titan-Iapetus; the gate has now returned a robust, positive-control-validated negative twice (#574 Stage B: 0/15 under real eccentricity, root-caused to the #571 free-single-closure periodicity gap; #575: 0/6 under inclination alone, using the CORRECT #563 symmetric construction that made the Uranian family genuine). The #575 negative is trustworthy, not a broken filter: the same pipeline reproduces the Uranian golden bit-for-bit AND finds 9 coplanar Titan-Iapetus closures that DO repeat to machine precision -- it can find genuine repeating cyclers when they exist. The near-misses are robustly infeasible, not tantalizing: the Omega+tof-free continuation closes a single cycle to machine precision but then diverges by ~2.44M km (2x Titan's orbital radius) over 3 cycles; the zero-tof-drift Omega-only variant leaves a ~6-21 m/s per-cycle discontinuity it cannot null -- a ~12-order-of-magnitude gap from the Uranian coplanar 4e-15 km/s. PHYSICAL ROOT CAUSE (honest, different-regime): Umbriel-Oberon (#312) both orbit within ~0.1-0.15deg of Uranus's equatorial/Laplace plane -> MUTUAL inclination ~0.1-0.2deg, so the circular-COPLANAR idealized model is NEAR-EXACT for them and the inclination-extension is a negligible correction. Titan-Iapetus have a MUTUAL inclination of ~15deg (Titan near Saturn's Laplace plane; Iapetus far out at a=3.56M km near its own local Laplace plane tilted ~15.5deg in the reference frame used) -> the coplanar idealized model was NEVER a good starting point and inclination is a LEADING-ORDER effect, not a correction. The coplanar symmetric family is periodic by a mirror symmetry about the common orbital plane; a 15deg tilt breaks that symmetry except in a node-locked configuration (encounters at the mutual line of nodes), which is generically incompatible with the commensurate-timing condition at the ~4.975 non-resonant period ratio (Stage 1b's Omega->{0,180deg} is the search reaching for that node alignment and failing to null the residual). A genuine inclined symmetric family would therefore be a codimension-higher, structurally DISCONNECTED family that continuation from coplanar seeds cannot reach. CONDITIONALITY / RE-OPEN RULE: this negative is honestly conditional on the SEARCH METHOD (continuation from circular seeds). The one method not tried is a from-scratch node-locked inclined symmetric construction -- essentially the #552 general 3D/inclined build that was explicitly killed on 2026-07-10. It is recorded here as the named, licensed, strictly-more-capable re-open method but is NOT dispatched: its expected yield is low (even a marginal node-locked inclined family must still survive the #574 eccentricity kill-gate + Saturn V2-V4-strict gauntlet that already killed 0/15 of the #571 population, with Titan-Iapetus eccentricity perturbation 7-25x stronger than Uranus's negligible effect), it is genuine new capability not reuse, and it is not novelty-eligible -- building the very thing the #572->#575 gate was designed to gate, to chase a low-probability marginal result with no catalogue standing, would invert the disciplined structure of this thread. This stays quasi_cycler-CLASS evidence about our own idealized search space (same standing as #312), NOT a novelty claim and NOT a ballistic-cycler finding.
reopen_condition: A from-scratch node-locked inclined symmetric construction that searches directly AT Iapetus's real inclination for encounters at the mutual line of nodes (a family structurally disconnected from the coplanar symmetric family, hence unreachable by the continuation method used here). NOT built and NOT dispatched as of 2026-07-12 (would re-open the explicitly-killed #552 general 3D/inclined moontour build). Re-check this pair cheaply IF such a capability is independently built for another system.
